purpose: Explains dollar overvaluation through technology investment inflows independent of rate differentials
components: Technology investment inflows tracked via TIC data ($40-60B/month)
EPFR fund flow data ($300B+ into US tech funds)
Dot-com parallel (1997-2001): DXY +25% when Fed not systematically tighter
Captive flow premium (3-5% of overvaluation) from US-China tech bifurcation
Monitorable signposts: capex-to-revenue ratio, foreign equity ownership, DXY-PPP deviation
current_assessment: The capex dollar is currently the dominant FX regime factor, operating orthogonally to rate differentials. This explains why DXY remains elevated despite 100-150bp of Fed cuts.
limitations: n=1 historical analogue (dot-com) with different fiscal/geopolitical backdrop
Dollar-tech correlation may reflect common factors, not causal chain
Technology landscape evolves rapidly — European/Asian alternatives could gain share
